homepage.heroSubline: Precision demolition for Texas' toughest projects. Serving Houston since 2012 with locally proven methods.
aboutText: Legacy Demolition started with one truck and a commitment to honest work. Twelve years later, we've torn down everything from Montrose bungalows to Katy McMansions.
We don't just knock things down—we plan every job for Houston's unique conditions. Humid summers demand careful material handling, and flood risks require strategic debris management.
When hurricanes hit Houston, we're first on site clearing storm damage. Our team lives here, works here, and rebuilds here.

valueProps.title: Local Know-How
valueProps.description: We understand Houston's soil conditions, building codes, and weather challenges better than out-of-town crews.

valueProps.title: No Hidden Costs
valueProps.description: Fixed-price quotes include Houston's 7.25% sales tax and disposal fees—no surprises when the bill comes.

valueProps.title: Storm-Ready
valueProps.description: 24/7 emergency response when hurricanes flood Houston homes. We've cleared 200+ storm-damaged properties since 2017.

valueProps.title: Debris Accountability
valueProps.description: Every load tracked to approved Houston-area landfills. No illegal dumping in our city.

homepage.ctaSubline: Call 555-DEM-RQ18 for same-day Houston demolition estimates.

services.name: Residential Demolition
services.shortDescription: Complete home teardowns with Houston-approved disposal.
services.description:
From aging Heights bungalows to flood-damaged Pearland homes, we handle full residential demolitions the Houston way.

Houston's clay soil requires specialized foundation removal—we excavate 18" deeper than code to prevent future settling. Summer humidity warps materials unpredictably; our crews secure unstable structures before demolition begins.

Every job includes asbestos testing (common in 1970s Houston builds) and proper disposal at Harris County-approved facilities. We salvage reusable materials for local Habitat for Humanity projects.
process: Step 1: Houston-specific site survey (checks for flood zone rules, overhead wires, clay soil conditions)
Step 2: Permitting (we handle all City of Houston paperwork)
Step 3: Utility disconnection by licensed Houston providers
Step 4: Demolition with debris sorted for recycling
benefits: Clay soil expertise prevents future property issues
Full compliance with Houston floodplain regulations
No surprise costs—Houston taxes included upfront

faq.question: How long does a Houston home demolition take?
faq.answer: Most single-family homes take 2-3 days. Add 1 day for homes in flood zones requiring special debris handling per Harris County rules.

faq.question: Do you handle asbestos in older Houston homes?
faq.answer: Yes—we test all pre-1980 structures and use licensed Houston abatement crews when needed. This is included in your quote.

services.name: Commercial Demolition
services.shortDescription: Strip-outs and full teardowns for Houston businesses.
services.description:
Houston's commercial spaces need specialized demolition—we've cleared everything from Midtown restaurants to industrial warehouses near the Ship Channel.

Modern Houston office buildings often require phased demolition to avoid disrupting adjacent businesses. We use noise-dampening equipment in dense areas like the Medical Center. For strip-outs, we separate materials to maximize recycling rebates at Houston processing centers.

All projects include dust control measures critical for Houston's humidity. Our team knows which materials require special handling under Texas environmental regulations.
process: Step 1: Houston business license verification
Step 2: Traffic control plan for urban sites
Step 3: Hazardous material survey (common in older Houston industrial sites)
Step 4: Demolition with daily debris removal
benefits: Minimizes business disruption in high-traffic areas
Maximizes recycling rebates for Houston businesses
Fully insured for urban demolition risks

faq.question: Can you demolish part of a Houston strip center?
faq.Answer: Yes—we use temporary barriers and work off-hours to minimize impact on neighboring businesses. Most retail demolitions complete in 72 hours.

services.name: Concrete Removal
services.shortDescription: Driveway, patio, and foundation removal for Houston properties.
services.description:
Houston's extreme weather destroys concrete faster than most cities. We remove and replace damaged slabs with Texas-tough methods.

Summer heat causes concrete to buckle—we use infrared scanning to identify weakened sections before removal. Winter freezes (though rare) create subsurface cracks; our team checks for hidden damage during demo.

All concrete gets crushed at our Houston-area facility for reuse as road base. We handle permitting for sidewalk replacements to meet City of Houston ADA standards.
process: Step 1: Scan slab for hidden voids (common in Houston's expansive soil)
Step 2: Permitting for sidewalk/curb work
Step 3: Breakup with minimal vibration (protects Houston's shallow water lines)
Step 4: Haul to local recycling center
benefits: Prevents trip hazards on uneven Houston sidewalks

faq.question: Why does Houston concrete fail so quickly?
faq.Answer: Our humidity causes rapid corrosion of rebar, while clay soil shifts undermine slabs. We install replacement concrete with Houston-specific reinforcement.

services.name: Interior Demolition
services.description:
Houston's humidity demands careful interior demolition—we prevent mold spread during kitchen/bath remodels.

Older homes in neighborhoods like Montrose often contain lead paint and plaster. Our containment systems exceed Houston health department standards. For condo remodels, we coordinate with building managers to meet high-rise disposal rules.

We salvage historic materials from Houston's 1920s-50s homes whenever possible. All debris leaves the site daily—critical for urban properties with limited storage space.
process: Step 1: Hazardous material testing
Step 2: Containment system installation
Step 3: Selective demolition by hand
Step 4: Daily debris removal
benefits: Works around Houston's common pier-and-beam foundations

faq.question: How do you control dust in Houston homes?
faq.Answer: We use negative air machines with HEPA filters—essential for Houston's allergen-heavy environment. Plastic barriers contain every work area.

services.name: Pool Removal
services.shortDescription: Complete pool demolitions for Houston homeowners.
services.description:
Houston's high water table makes pool removal complex—we've filled 300+ pools across Harris County without drainage issues.

Vinyl-lined pools in The Woodlands require different removal than Houston's common gunite pools. We compact fill dirt in layers to prevent future settling in our clay soil. All permits include Harris County groundwater protection checks.

Our process prevents mosquito breeding—a critical concern in Houston's humid climate. We leave your yard ready for landscaping or expansion.
process: Step 1: Water drainage plan (varies by Houston neighborhood)
Step 2: Structural demolition
Step 3: Soil compaction testing
Step 4: Final grading
benefits: Prevents Houston's common pool collapse hazards

faq.question: Why do Houston pools need special removal?
faq.Answer: Our high water table can float empty pools out of the ground. We drill relief holes and use dense fill materials to prevent this.

reviews.name: James T.
reviews.text: Legacy tore down our flood-damaged Meyerland home fast. They knew exactly how to handle the mold issues from Houston humidity and had the debris gone before the next rain.

reviews.name: Lisa R.
reviews.text: Our Montrose bungalow had layers of lead paint. The crew sealed off everything and even salvaged the original heart pine floors. Zero dust in the rest of the house—impressive for Houston's allergen season.

reviews.name: Carlos M.
reviews.text: They removed our buckled Katy driveway in one day and explained why Houston's soil caused the damage. The recycled concrete rebate was a nice bonus.

reviews.name: Diane K.
reviews.text: Legacy demolished our Midtown restaurant with zero disruption to neighboring businesses. Their dust control was essential with Houston health inspectors nearby.

reviews.name: Marcus W.
reviews.text: Our Woodlands pool was a safety hazard after the freeze. They filled it properly for our clay soil and even graded the yard for new landscaping. No drainage issues two seasons later.

reviews.name: Sarah P.
reviews.text: They salvaged bricks from our 1950s Heights home for reuse and handled all the Houston permits while we dealt with insurance. The site was cleaner after demolition than before.

serviceAreas.content: Legacy Demolition proudly serves Sugar Land, TX, a thriving city known for its master-planned communities built primarily in the 1990s and 2000s. With a population of around 111,000, Sugar Land offers a mix of suburban charm and modern amenities. Our team specializes in residential and commercial demolition, ensuring safe and efficient removal of structures to make way for new developments or renovations. Given the hot, humid summers, we prioritize weather-resistant demolition techniques to minimize disruptions.
serviceAreas.localContext: Sugar Land features well-planned neighborhoods with lush landscaping and community-focused amenities.

serviceAreas.content: Katy, TX, is a rapidly growing city with newer planned communities developed from the 2000s to the 2020s. With a population of approximately 21,000, Katy combines small-town charm with modern conveniences. The hot, humid climate requires careful planning, and our team ensures projects are completed efficiently while adhering to local regulations.
